DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF VICTORIA HANIFF TO PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL VERIFIED RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, NUMBERS 1 TO 50, AND TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DEFENDANT'S FIRST REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF VICTORIA HANIFF'S RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION ARE INADEQUATE. WITHIN 14 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF VICTORIA HANIFF SHALL SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL, VERIFIED RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S FIRST REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, NUMBERS 1 TO 50, AND SHALL PRODUCE TO DEFENDANT ALL NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF VICTORIA HANIFF SHALL PREPARE A PRIVILEGE LOG IDENTIFYING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WHICH SHE IS WITHHOLDING FROM PRODUCTION, AND THE BASIS ON WHICH THOSE DOCUMENTS ARE BEING WITHHELD. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: MARGARET NIVER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

DEFENDANTS 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, MATTHIAS SPANLE, MARK SHER, PAMELA YOON'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement

Defendants' motion to enforce settlement agreement is denied. Per the addendum to the settlement agreement, the "HOA through the ... property manager shall retain a surveyor to survey the parking spaces in the west garage." Defendants have not presented any evidence that a survey was done. While Mr. Spanle stated in his declaration that the "surveyor marked the lines precisely as marked on the recorded map," there is no evidence that such marking constituted the "survey" required by the settlement agreement. Moreover, no evidence was provided by the surveyor of what it did or could have done to comply with the terms of the settlement agreement.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Off Calendar May-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, MATTHIAS SPANLE, MARK SHER, PAMELA YOON's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement.

Defendants' motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted in substantial part. The $50,000 on deposit with the court will be released to defendant 830 Lake Street Homeowner's Association, but may be used only to pay for the services of an "independent board" or an "independent property manager." The court is aware that there is no independent board and may never be one. The court defines "independent property manager" as a person or company in the business of managing condominium properties in San Francisco and has no business or personal connection with any of the owners of the units prior to the person or company's hiring or the date of the settlement agreement, whichever is later. This interpretation of the settlement agreement comports with both the language of the agreement and the intent of the parties. Defendants' request for fees and costs is denied. Paragraph 8.2 of the settlement agreement does not provide for prevailing party fees in a dispute about the $50,000.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Charles, Esq. 415-399-2900 of Peterson Martin Reynolds for Plaintiffs Victoria Haniff and Catherine Haniff who are also both present.
Todd Angstadt, Esq. 415-278-9400 of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Plaintff argues the HOA never executed the agreement. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, MATTHIAS SPANLE, MARK SHER, PAMELA YOON Defendants' Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement.

Defendants 830 Lake Street Homeowners' Association, Matthias Spanle, Mark Sher, and Pamela Yoon's motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted. The court has jurisdiction to enforce the settlement per CCP 664.6. Defendants shall pay the settlement funds by February 28, 2018. The parties were obligated merely to "explore" in good faith the concept of retaining an independent board. In other words, the parties agreed to study or investigate the possibility of retaining an independent board. An e-mail from plaintiff's counsel that contemplated that the parties might want a property management company if an independent board could not be procured comports with this interpretation. (Halvorsen Dec. Ex. B.) The declarations of the defendants demonstrate that they explored the possibility of an independent board in good faith and satisfied that condition of the settlement. The parties shall perform the remaining non-monetary items of their settlement by March 31, 2018. Plaintiffs' argument that the settlement is unenforceable lacks merit. While the "HOA" never executed the agreement, the three individual defendants that constitute 4 of the 6 ownership interests in the HOA agreed to the settlement. There is nothing precluding them from ratifying the settlement on behalf of the HOA. In addition, Mr. Spanle was an elected member of the board when the settlement was executed and he signed the agreement. Plaintiffs also fail to show that there was no mutual assent or that the settlement constitutes an unenforceable agreement to agree. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
